Per-batter detail

1.Kazuma OkamotoRHBTOR vs Casey Mize (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.541 / xBA 0.321 / xSLG 0.694 (+226 pts)K 27.0%BB 8.6%HR 14.3%Hit 33.5%Strong Hitter

Kazuma Okamoto RHB vs Casey Mize RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 789+0+0, pitcher rows 473+2473+1790

Verdict

Mize's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Kazuma Okamoto should look to drive the split-finger. Projection: 0.541 xwOBA (+226 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -14 pts (raw 0.493 -> adj 0.479); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +62 pts (bb_type-adj 0.479 -> final 0.541); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

bat speed 71.1 mph
swing length 7.9 ft
attack angle 14.6°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.6+11.635+7.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-5.1+12.643+7.5swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slider-7.0+18.716+11.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

2.Matt VierlingRHBDET vs Mason Fluharty (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.521 / xBA 0.344 / xSLG 0.830 (+206 pts)K 16.8%BB 8.9%HR 3.6%Hit 36.9%Strong Hitter

Matt Vierling RHB vs Mason Fluharty LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 467+436+2457, pitcher rows 284+1017+0

Verdict

Fluharty's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Matt Vierling should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.521 xwOBA (+206 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -8 pts (raw 0.523 -> adj 0.515); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.515 -> final 0.521); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Matt Vierling is on a 14-day heater (+43 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Cutter67.40.5880.284GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 8%0.586-20.5550.3260.9270.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

3.James WoodLHBWSH vs Chris Bassitt (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.495 / xBA 0.304 / xSLG 0.617 (+180 pts)K 28.3%BB 15.5%HR 6.5%Hit 31.2%Strong Hitter

James Wood LHB vs Chris Bassitt RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 892+2923+1406, pitcher rows 703+2958+2976

Verdict

Bassitt's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while James Wood should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.495 xwOBA (+180 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +57 pts (bb_type-adj 0.439 -> final 0.495); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

4.Corbin CarrollLHBAZ vs Tomoyuki Sugano (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.488 / xBA 0.345 / xSLG 0.722 (+173 pts)K 14.8%BB 8.4%HR 7.6%Hit 32.0%Strong Hitter

Corbin Carroll LHB vs Tomoyuki Sugano RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 670+2514+2649, pitcher rows 659+2573+0

Verdict

Sugano's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Corbin Carroll should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.488 xwOBA (+173 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +15 pts (raw 0.455 -> adj 0.470); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +18 pts (bb_type-adj 0.470 -> final 0.488); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Tomoyuki Sugano is in a 14-day rough patch (+105 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 73.4 mph
swing length 7.3 ft
attack angle 9.8°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Split-Finger-7.6+13.540+5.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
4-Seam Fastball-4.8+6.1246+1.3on plane
Curveball-10.1+13.658+3.5swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Sweeper-7.7+11.535+3.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slider-8.5+13.393+4.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

5.Jeremiah JacksonRHBBAL vs Cade Cavalli (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.479 / xBA 0.450 / xSLG 0.576 (+164 pts)K 17.4%BB 3.3%HR 6.6%Hit 39.1%Strong Hitter

Jeremiah Jackson RHB vs Cade Cavalli RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 468+707+0, pitcher rows 672+790+0

Verdict

Cavalli's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jeremiah Jackson should look to drive the knuckle curve. Projection: 0.479 xwOBA (+164 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +22 pts (raw 0.427 -> adj 0.449); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +30 pts (bb_type-adj 0.449 -> final 0.479); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jeremiah Jackson is on a 14-day slump (-83 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

6.Jonathan ArandaLHBTB vs Sandy Alcantara (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.468 / xBA 0.313 / xSLG 0.636 (+153 pts)K 16.0%BB 11.7%HR 5.2%Hit 33.9%Strong Hitter

Jonathan Aranda LHB vs Sandy Alcantara RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 747+1732+574, pitcher rows 865+2893+0

Verdict

Projection: 0.468 xwOBA (+153 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.444 -> adj 0.433); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +35 pts (bb_type-adj 0.433 -> final 0.468); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jonathan Aranda is on a 14-day heater (+58 pts vs season). Sandy Alcantara is in a 14-day rough patch (+49 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

8.Iván HerreraRHBSTL vs Noah Cameron (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.444 / xBA 0.312 / xSLG 0.589 (+129 pts)K 13.3%BB 14.1%HR 6.7%Hit 28.6%Strong Hitter

Iván Herrera RHB vs Noah Cameron LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 763+1813+1036, pitcher rows 600+2167+0

Verdict

Cameron's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Iván Herrera should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.444 xwOBA (+129 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.444 -> adj 0.431); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.431 -> final 0.444); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Iván Herrera is on a 14-day slump (-73 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

McDonald's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Nick Kurtz should look to drive the changeup. Projection: 0.443 xwOBA (+128 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +25 pts (bb_type-adj 0.418 -> final 0.443); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Count-state blend: +19 pts (bb_type-adj 0.424 -> final 0.443); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Sweeper1.80.3310.377GB 41% / LD 26% / FB 26% / PU 7%0.315-160.3770.3530.36111.2

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

11.Jo AdellRHBLAA vs Justin Wrobleski (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.442 / xBA 0.317 / xSLG 0.604 (+127 pts)K 20.7%BB 5.0%HR 10.1%Hit 29.2%Strong Hitter

Jo Adell RHB vs Justin Wrobleski LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 718+2221+1845, pitcher rows 618+1175+593

Verdict

Wrobleski's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jo Adell should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.442 xwOBA (+127 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -19 pts (raw 0.426 -> adj 0.407); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +35 pts (bb_type-adj 0.407 -> final 0.442); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jo Adell is on a 14-day heater (+61 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

12.Aaron JudgeRHBNYY vs Huascar Brazobán (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.430 / xBA 0.293 / xSLG 0.559 (+115 pts)K 28.0%BB 10.9%HR 6.0%Hit 27.0%Strong Hitter

Aaron Judge RHB vs Huascar Brazobán RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 844+2875+3235, pitcher rows 325+1017+903

Verdict

Projection: 0.430 xwOBA (+115 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -59 pts (raw 0.462 -> adj 0.403); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +27 pts (bb_type-adj 0.403 -> final 0.430); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Aaron Judge is on a 14-day slump (-81 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

13.Cole YoungLHBSEA vs Walker Buehler (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.430 / xBA 0.330 / xSLG 0.553 (+115 pts)K 13.6%BB 12.8%HR 1.3%Hit 31.8%Strong Hitter

Cole Young LHB vs Walker Buehler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 698+1039+0, pitcher rows 634+2191+1599

Verdict

Buehler's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Cole Young should look to drive the knuckle curve. Projection: 0.430 xwOBA (+115 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +28 pts (raw 0.411 -> adj 0.439); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.439 -> final 0.430); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Cole Young is on a 14-day slump (-60 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 69.8 mph
swing length 6.7 ft
attack angle 6.3°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.2+4.7132+0.5on plane
Cutter-5.2+2.927-2.3on plane
Knuckle Curve-10.3+6.3*2-4.0swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Changeup-6.5+10.837+4.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-5.1+1.935-3.2swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Slider-7.0+10.022+3.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

14.Kyle SchwarberLHBPHI vs Bubba Chandler (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.430 / xBA 0.231 / xSLG 0.626 (+115 pts)K 26.9%BB 15.5%HR 10.4%Hit 21.5%Strong Hitter

Kyle Schwarber LHB vs Bubba Chandler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 813+3157+2950, pitcher rows 679+461+0

Verdict

Chandler's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Kyle Schwarber should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.430 xwOBA (+115 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.410 -> adj 0.397); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +32 pts (bb_type-adj 0.397 -> final 0.430); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 69.1 mph
swing length 6.7 ft
attack angle 7.7°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.9+4.536-0.4on plane
Curveball-9.9+7.7*2-2.3on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

16.Josh NaylorLHBSEA vs Walker Buehler (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.425 / xBA 0.362 / xSLG 0.547 (+110 pts)K 9.9%BB 9.9%HR 5.9%Hit 30.4%Strong Hitter

Josh Naylor LHB vs Walker Buehler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 597+2196+2463, pitcher rows 634+2191+1599

Verdict

Buehler's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Josh Naylor should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.425 xwOBA (+110 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +24 pts (raw 0.395 -> adj 0.420); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.420 -> final 0.425); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

17.Carter JensenLHBKC vs Kyle Leahy (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.419 / xBA 0.278 / xSLG 0.479 (+104 pts)K 28.5%BB 17.7%HR 5.0%Hit 23.6%Strong Hitter

Carter Jensen LHB vs Kyle Leahy RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 621+293+0, pitcher rows 690+1352+695

Verdict

Leahy's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Carter Jensen should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.419 xwOBA (+104 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -23 pts (raw 0.405 -> adj 0.382); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +37 pts (bb_type-adj 0.382 -> final 0.419); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Carter Jensen is on a 14-day slump (-66 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 72.7 mph
swing length 6.9 ft
attack angle 3.6°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.6-0.271-4.8swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Curveball-9.4+7.112-2.3on plane
Changeup-7.0+9.625+2.5on plane

Sinker-5.2-1.416-6.6swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

18.Michael BuschLHBCHC vs Davis Martin (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.416 / xBA 0.287 / xSLG 0.573 (+101 pts)K 19.8%BB 11.5%HR 6.7%Hit 25.9%Strong Hitter

Michael Busch LHB vs Davis Martin RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 723+2419+2369, pitcher rows 733+2315+892

Verdict

Martin's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Michael Busch should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.416 xwOBA (+101 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.413 -> adj 0.399); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +17 pts (bb_type-adj 0.399 -> final 0.416); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Michael Busch is on a 14-day heater (+74 pts vs season). Davis Martin is in a 14-day hot stretch (-68 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Luis García Jr. LHB vs Chris Bassitt RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 453+1842+1890, pitcher rows 703+2958+2976

Verdict

Bassitt's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Luis García Jr. should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.411 xwOBA (+96 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.402 -> final 0.411); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Luis García Jr. is on a 14-day heater (+41 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

bat speed 70.7 mph
swing length 7.3 ft
attack angle 9.3°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sinker-5.9+7.779+1.8on plane
Cutter-4.8+6.547+1.8on plane
Curveball-10.6+13.076+2.4on plane

Sweeper-8.5+12.836+4.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

20.Shohei OhtaniLHBLAD vs José Soriano (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.410 / xBA 0.259 / xSLG 0.468 (+95 pts)K 22.9%BB 15.3%HR 7.1%Hit 22.9%Edge Hitter

Shohei Ohtani LHB vs José Soriano RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 734+3241+3152, pitcher rows 764+2784+1743

Verdict

Soriano's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Shohei Ohtani should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.410 xwOBA (+95 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -60 pts (raw 0.419 -> adj 0.359); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +51 pts (bb_type-adj 0.359 -> final 0.410); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Shohei Ohtani is on a 14-day slump (-64 pts vs season). José Soriano is in a 14-day rough patch (+82 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

21.Julio RodríguezRHBSEA vs Walker Buehler (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.409 / xBA 0.294 / xSLG 0.559 (+94 pts)K 18.0%BB 5.7%HR 5.2%Hit 30.3%Edge Hitter

Julio Rodríguez RHB vs Walker Buehler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 752+2727+2389, pitcher rows 634+2191+1599

Verdict

Buehler's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Julio Rodríguez should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.409 xwOBA (+94 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +5 pts (raw 0.386 -> adj 0.391); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +18 pts (bb_type-adj 0.391 -> final 0.409); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

22.Bobby Witt Jr.RHBKC vs Kyle Leahy (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.407 / xBA 0.321 / xSLG 0.566 (+92 pts)K 15.2%BB 3.6%HR 5.2%Hit 32.0%Edge Hitter

Bobby Witt Jr. RHB vs Kyle Leahy RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 745+2542+2725, pitcher rows 690+1352+695

Verdict

Leahy's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Bobby Witt Jr. should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.407 xwOBA (+92 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +8 pts (raw 0.386 -> adj 0.394); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.394 -> final 0.407); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 23.CJ AbramsLHBWSH vs Chris Bassitt (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.405 / xBA 0.307 / xSLG 0.510 (+90 pts)K 15.4%BB 8.4%HR 5.0%Hit 27.3%Edge Hitter

CJ Abrams LHB vs Chris Bassitt RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 709+2324+2273, pitcher rows 703+2958+2976

Verdict

Bassitt's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while CJ Abrams should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.405 xwOBA (+90 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +19 pts (bb_type-adj 0.386 -> final 0.405); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. CJ Abrams is on a 14-day slump (-60 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

24.Richie PalaciosLHBTB vs Sandy Alcantara (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.403 / xBA 0.300 / xSLG 0.476 (+88 pts)K 15.3%BB 15.4%HR 2.3%Hit 28.1%Edge Hitter

Richie Palacios LHB vs Sandy Alcantara RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 307+190+1286, pitcher rows 865+2893+0

Verdict

Alcantara's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Richie Palacios should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.403 xwOBA (+88 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +12 pts (raw 0.380 -> adj 0.393); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.393 -> final 0.403); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Sandy Alcantara is in a 14-day rough patch (+49 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

25.Travis BazzanaLHBCLE vs Chris Paddack (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.401 / xBA 0.317 / xSLG 0.461 (+86 pts)K 9.4%BB 14.6%HR 0.0%Hit 29.6%Edge Hitter

Travis Bazzana LHB vs Chris Paddack RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 271+0+0, pitcher rows 585+2566+1462

Verdict

Paddack's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Travis Bazzana should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.401 xwOBA (+86 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: -8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.409 -> final 0.401); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

26.Randy ArozarenaRHBSEA vs Walker Buehler (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.400 / xBA 0.252 / xSLG 0.492 (+85 pts)K 21.7%BB 9.8%HR 4.0%Hit 24.2%Edge Hitter

Randy Arozarena RHB vs Walker Buehler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 757+3112+2776, pitcher rows 634+2191+1599

Verdict

Buehler's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Randy Arozarena should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.400 xwOBA (+85 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.365 -> adj 0.374); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +26 pts (bb_type-adj 0.374 -> final 0.400); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

27.Vinnie PasquantinoLHBKC vs Kyle Leahy (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.398 / xBA 0.317 / xSLG 0.528 (+83 pts)K 12.2%BB 11.3%HR 5.2%Hit 26.8%Edge Hitter

Vinnie Pasquantino LHB vs Kyle Leahy RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 713+2588+2240, pitcher rows 690+1352+695

Verdict

Leahy's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Vinnie Pasquantino should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.398 xwOBA (+83 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.402 -> adj 0.395); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

28.Colson MontgomeryLHBCWS vs Jameson Taillon (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.396 / xBA 0.242 / xSLG 0.516 (+81 pts)K 28.5%BB 9.0%HR 7.8%Hit 25.4%Edge Hitter

Colson Montgomery LHB vs Jameson Taillon RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 655+1088+0, pitcher rows 744+2114+2483

Verdict

Taillon's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Colson Montgomery should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.396 xwOBA (+81 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.375 -> adj 0.367); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +30 pts (bb_type-adj 0.367 -> final 0.396); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 75.5 mph
swing length 7.7 ft
attack angle 11.5°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.9+4.1113-0.8on plane
Curveball-9.9+20.320+10.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Changeup-7.6+15.457+7.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-6.6+12.834+6.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-5.7+6.424+0.7on plane
Sweeper-8.7+19.312+10.6swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Verdict

Cavalli's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Tyler O'Neill should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.396 xwOBA (+81 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.376 -> adj 0.367); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +29 pts (bb_type-adj 0.367 -> final 0.396); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

30.Pete Crow-ArmstrongLHBCHC vs Davis Martin (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.395 / xBA 0.301 / xSLG 0.539 (+80 pts)K 18.7%BB 5.9%HR 5.4%Hit 29.2%Edge Hitter

Pete Crow-Armstrong LHB vs Davis Martin RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 641+2403+1424, pitcher rows 733+2315+892

Verdict

Projection: 0.395 xwOBA (+80 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.374 -> final 0.395); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Davis Martin is in a 14-day hot stretch (-68 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

31.Kyle StowersLHBMIA vs Nick Martinez (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.394 / xBA 0.272 / xSLG 0.525 (+79 pts)K 25.3%BB 6.7%HR 6.6%Hit 27.7%Edge Hitter

Kyle Stowers LHB vs Nick Martinez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 333+1724+821, pitcher rows 669+2603+2126

Verdict

Martinez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Kyle Stowers should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.394 xwOBA (+79 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.374 -> adj 0.361); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +33 pts (bb_type-adj 0.361 -> final 0.394); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

32.Brendan DonovanLHBSEA vs Walker Buehler (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.393 / xBA 0.324 / xSLG 0.476 (+78 pts)K 8.1%BB 10.0%HR 3.5%Hit 26.9%Edge Hitter

Brendan Donovan LHB vs Walker Buehler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 323+1933+2487, pitcher rows 634+2191+1599

Verdict

Buehler's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Brendan Donovan should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.393 xwOBA (+78 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.384 -> adj 0.393); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Verdict

Severino's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Rafael Devers should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.393 xwOBA (+78 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +15 pts (bb_type-adj 0.377 -> final 0.393); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

34.Lawrence ButlerLHBATH vs Trevor McDonald (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.392 / xBA 0.288 / xSLG 0.519 (+77 pts)K 21.6%BB 8.2%HR 5.8%Hit 27.6%Edge Hitter

Lawrence Butler LHB vs Trevor McDonald RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 582+2521+1816, pitcher rows 182+213+39

Verdict

McDonald's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Lawrence Butler should look to drive the changeup. Projection: 0.392 xwOBA (+77 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.384 -> final 0.392); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Jake Burger RHB vs Kai-Wei Teng RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 601+1324+2089, pitcher rows 416+573+204

Verdict

Teng's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Jake Burger should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.391 xwOBA (+76 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +28 pts (bb_type-adj 0.362 -> final 0.391); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jake Burger is on a 14-day heater (+72 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

36.Tyler SoderstromLHBATH vs Trevor McDonald (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.391 / xBA 0.305 / xSLG 0.473 (+76 pts)K 17.8%BB 7.7%HR 3.8%Hit 27.6%Edge Hitter

Tyler Soderstrom LHB vs Trevor McDonald RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 641+2238+773, pitcher rows 182+213+39

Verdict

McDonald's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Tyler Soderstrom should look to drive the changeup. Projection: 0.391 xwOBA (+76 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.377 -> final 0.391); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

37.Ketel MarteRHBAZ vs Tomoyuki Sugano (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.389 / xBA 0.270 / xSLG 0.537 (+74 pts)K 11.1%BB 6.4%HR 6.1%Hit 28.0%Edge Hitter

Ketel Marte RHB vs Tomoyuki Sugano RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 620+2117+2175, pitcher rows 659+2573+0

Verdict

Sugano's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Ketel Marte should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.389 xwOBA (+74 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.382 -> final 0.389); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ketel Marte is on a 14-day slump (-75 pts vs season). Tomoyuki Sugano is in a 14-day rough patch (+105 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

38.Pete AlonsoRHBBAL vs Cade Cavalli (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.388 / xBA 0.280 / xSLG 0.475 (+73 pts)K 17.7%BB 8.8%HR 5.7%Hit 24.3%Edge Hitter

Pete Alonso RHB vs Cade Cavalli RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 767+2825+3049, pitcher rows 672+790+0

Verdict

Cavalli's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Pete Alonso should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.388 xwOBA (+73 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -25 pts (raw 0.386 -> adj 0.361); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +27 pts (bb_type-adj 0.361 -> final 0.388); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

40.José TenaLHBWSH vs Chris Bassitt (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.386 / xBA 0.298 / xSLG 0.492 (+71 pts)K 21.8%BB 8.9%HR 0.9%Hit 30.6%Edge Hitter

José Tena LHB vs Chris Bassitt RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 353+678+674, pitcher rows 703+2958+2976

Verdict

Bassitt's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while José Tena should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.386 xwOBA (+71 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.372 -> final 0.386); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. José Tena is on a 14-day heater (+104 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

41.Yordan AlvarezLHBHOU vs Jacob deGrom (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.385 / xBA 0.256 / xSLG 0.571 (+70 pts)K 22.8%BB 7.3%HR 6.3%Hit 27.1%Edge Hitter

Yordan Alvarez LHB vs Jacob deGrom RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 768+824+2359, pitcher rows 712+2617+172

Verdict

deGrom's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Yordan Alvarez should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.385 xwOBA (+70 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -40 pts (raw 0.404 -> adj 0.364); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.364 -> final 0.385); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Yordan Alvarez is on a 14-day slump (-63 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

42.Michael MasseyLHBKC vs Kyle Leahy (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.385 / xBA 0.327 / xSLG 0.498 (+70 pts)K 13.7%BB 5.7%HR 4.8%Hit 30.9%Edge Hitter

Michael Massey LHB vs Kyle Leahy RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 334+1013+1439, pitcher rows 690+1352+695

Verdict

Leahy's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Michael Massey should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.385 xwOBA (+70 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +35 pts (raw 0.337 -> adj 0.372); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.372 -> final 0.385); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

43.Wilyer AbreuLHBBOS vs Bryce Elder (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.385 / xBA 0.279 / xSLG 0.490 (+70 pts)K 23.7%BB 11.6%HR 5.1%Hit 25.8%Edge Hitter

Wilyer Abreu LHB vs Bryce Elder RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 686+1641+1806, pitcher rows 784+2519+864

Verdict

Elder's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Wilyer Abreu should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.385 xwOBA (+70 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +17 pts (bb_type-adj 0.367 -> final 0.385); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 44.Jarred KelenicLHBCWS vs Jameson Taillon (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.384 / xBA 0.263 / xSLG 0.521 (+69 pts)K 27.4%BB 8.0%HR 3.8%Hit 28.7%Edge Hitter

Jarred Kelenic LHB vs Jameson Taillon RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 135+258+1762, pitcher rows 744+2114+2483

Verdict

Taillon's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jarred Kelenic should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.384 xwOBA (+69 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.349 -> adj 0.355); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +29 pts (bb_type-adj 0.355 -> final 0.384); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

45.Bryce HarperLHBPHI vs Bubba Chandler (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.384 / xBA 0.239 / xSLG 0.561 (+69 pts)K 17.7%BB 12.8%HR 6.0%Hit 23.3%Edge Hitter

Bryce Harper LHB vs Bubba Chandler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 673+2264+2593, pitcher rows 679+461+0

Verdict

Chandler's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Bryce Harper should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.384 xwOBA (+69 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -34 pts (raw 0.407 -> adj 0.373); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.373 -> final 0.384); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Sinker0.20.3590.318GB 39% / LD 22% / FB 29% / PU 10%0.374+150.3770.2250.4939.8

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Verdict

Prielipp's sinker is the projected matchup advantage, while Sal Frelick should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.383 xwOBA (+68 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +38 pts (raw 0.361 -> adj 0.399); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.399 -> final 0.383); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

47.Zach NetoRHBLAA vs Justin Wrobleski (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.382 / xBA 0.289 / xSLG 0.492 (+67 pts)K 23.5%BB 6.5%HR 5.0%Hit 27.8%Edge Hitter

Zach Neto RHB vs Justin Wrobleski LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 833+2197+2295, pitcher rows 618+1175+593

Verdict

Wrobleski's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach Neto should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.382 xwOBA (+67 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -18 pts (raw 0.361 -> adj 0.344); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +38 pts (bb_type-adj 0.344 -> final 0.382); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

49.Jac CaglianoneLHBKC vs Kyle Leahy (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.379 / xBA 0.278 / xSLG 0.467 (+64 pts)K 24.0%BB 12.3%HR 2.6%Hit 25.9%Edge Hitter

Jac Caglianone LHB vs Kyle Leahy RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 528+858+0, pitcher rows 690+1352+695

Verdict

Leahy's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jac Caglianone should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.379 xwOBA (+64 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +23 pts (raw 0.335 -> adj 0.358); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.358 -> final 0.379); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 76.0 mph
swing length 7.7 ft
attack angle 8.7°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.6+1.187-3.5swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Curveball-9.4+13.638+4.2swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Changeup-7.0+13.031+6.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slider-6.6+12.727+6.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sweeper-7.5+15.319+7.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-5.2+1.617-3.6swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

50.Kyle TuckerLHBLAD vs José Soriano (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.379 / xBA 0.287 / xSLG 0.451 (+64 pts)K 14.9%BB 17.4%HR 2.4%Hit 21.7%Edge Hitter

Kyle Tucker LHB vs José Soriano RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 686+2437+1390, pitcher rows 764+2784+1743

Verdict

Soriano's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Kyle Tucker should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.379 xwOBA (+64 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -30 pts (raw 0.402 -> adj 0.372); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.372 -> final 0.379); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. José Soriano is in a 14-day rough patch (+82 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 69.3 mph
swing length 7.4 ft
attack angle 10.0°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sinker-6.5+5.361-1.2on plane
Knuckle Curve-9.0+16.316+7.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Split-Finger-7.4+14.629+7.2swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
4-Seam Fastball-5.0+4.5181-0.5on plane
Slider-7.7+13.273+5.5swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).
